purpose: Govern mandatory turn-start loading, task resolution, and active-memory compilation for Meta-Runtime-Memory.
instruction: At turn start, ensure the durable store exists before any memory read.
Consume Codex session stream events and attribute the hook to the current session_id + turn_id before substantial execution.
Read ACTIVE_TASK.json, ACTIVE_RUNTIME.json, USER_MEMORY.json, and the active task TASK_MEMORY.json if one exists.
Persist SESSION_MEMORY.json plus TURN_AUDIT.json for the current session/turn and compile active memory into the runtime root.
If the active task is missing or stale relative to the current session turn, auto-bind the correct durable task before continuing.
workflow: Run init-store when the store has not been initialized yet.
Watch for task_started and user_message events in the Codex session log.
Resolve or auto-bind the active task pointer.
Load task-layer memory for the active task when available.
Write ACTIVE_RUNTIME.json and the turn-start TURN_AUDIT.json.
Continue the real task only after the compiled memory is available.
rules: Turn-start proof must include session_id + turn_id attribution.
Compiled active memory is a runtime artifact, not the durable source of truth.
If no task is active yet, user-layer memory still loads and task-layer memory remains null.
Do not substitute chat history for compiled memory.
